tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Get Compeller platform capabilities, supported features, and API version.

tools.name: get_pricing
tools.description: Get current Compeller subscription plans and pricing.

tools.name: list_styles
tools.description: List available visual styles for Compeller video generation.

tools.name: upload_media
tools.description: Get upload instructions for media files (audio, images, video). Returns the upload URL and required headers. Requires API token authentication.

tools.name: search_music
tools.description: Search Deezer preview tracks by song, artist, or album. Use this when the user provides a song string but no MP3/WAV/FLAC file.

tools.name: create_compel_from_music
tools.description: Create a compel from a selected Deezer preview track. If the user provides an audio file instead, upload that file and use create_compel.

tools.name: create_compel
tools.description: Create a Compeller generation job from primary audio media and optional reference media. Returns compel id, status, and links to track progress and retrieve renderings.

tools.name: get_compel
tools.description: Get the current status, progress, and details of a Compeller generation job.

tools.name: start_render
tools.description: Start rendering a READY compel with default configuration so an agent can continue to final MP4 without opening the browser UI.

tools.name: list_compels
tools.description: List all Compeller generation jobs for the authenticated account, newest first.

tools.name: search_compels
tools.description: Search Compeller generation jobs by title for the authenticated account.

tools.name: list_renderings
tools.description: List all video renderings for a specific compel.

tools.name: get_rendering
tools.description: Get details of a specific video rendering.

tools.name: search_media
tools.description: Search and list uploaded media files for the authenticated account.

tools.name: register_webhook
tools.description: Register an HTTPS endpoint to receive signed push notifications for compel lifecycle events (compel.ready when renderable, compel.completed, compel.failed). Returns webhook_id and the HMAC-SHA256 signing secret exactly once - store the secret immediately, it is never returned again. Deliveries are s

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description: List all webhook endpoints registered for the authenticated account. Secrets are never returned by this tool.

tools.name: delete_webhook
tools.description: Delete a webhook endpoint by id. Removes the registration and stops future deliveries.

tools.name: test_webhook_delivery
tools.description: Synchronously POST a synthetic webhook.test event to a registered endpoint. Uses the same HMAC-SHA256 signature as real deliveries, runs the standard URL safety check at delivery time, and returns {webhook_id, event_id, event_type, delivered, response_status, response_body_preview, latency_ms, error

tools.name: update_webhook
tools.description: Update one or more mutable fields on a registered webhook endpoint: url, events, active. At least one of these must be provided. Validation mirrors register_webhook (https-only, <= 2048 chars, URL safety blocklist). Returns the updated endpoint (secret is never returned by this tool - use rotate_web

tools.name: rotate_webhook_secret
tools.description: Mint a new HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for a registered webhook endpoint. The previous secret is invalidated immediately - integrators must update their receiver before the next compel terminal event fires. Returns the new secret exactly once; store it on receipt.

tools.name: cancel_compel
tools.description: Cancel an in-progress compel. Dequeues it if still queued, otherwise cancels any active external (AI provider) tasks and transitions it to CANCELLED. Idempotent - cancelling an already-CANCELLED compel succeeds. Use to stop a mis-prompted or runaway job before it consumes more render minutes.

tools.name: get_account_credits
tools.description: Get the authenticated account's render-minute balance and plan: minutes remaining (free + paid), monthly allowance, plan code, and whether the quota is exceeded. Call before starting an expensive render so an agent can make cost-aware decisions.

tools.name: list_lighting_effects
tools.description: List the COM-497 starter lighting effects available to launch as cues (e.g. "gobo-cycle", "x-move", "dimmer-pulse"). Each effect carries an id, the capabilities it needs, and a launch_contract template. Use the returned id as effect_id for launch_lighting_cue.

tools.name: launch_lighting_cue
tools.description: Launch a starter lighting effect onto a stage as a live cue (e.g. "gobo-cycle on the stage's 7R beams at speed 0.5"). Pass effect_id (from list_lighting_effects) and optional variables to override effect defaults. Validation is loud: an unknown effect, a launch_contract whose bindings a stage cannot

tools.name: list_lighting_cues
tools.description: List the live (non-stopped) lighting cues REACT is executing, including each cue's launch_id, resolved variables, launch_contract, and feedback. Optionally scope to one stage.

tools.name: get_lighting_cue
tools.description: Fetch a single live lighting cue by its launch_id, including resolved variables, launch_contract, and REACT feedback.

tools.name: stop_lighting_cue
tools.description: Stop a live cue by its launch_id. Idempotent - the cue is marked stop-requested so REACT drops it on its next refresh.

tools.name: set_lighting_cue_variables
tools.description: Update variables on a live cue (e.g. change cycle speed or intensity mid-show). Only the variables you pass change; the rest keep their current values. Stopped cues are rejected.

tools.name: ack_lighting_cue
tools.description: REACT/BAE acknowledgement for a launch_id. Idempotently records whether the executor accepted or rejected the cue and which executor observed it.

tools.name: report_lighting_cue
tools.description: REACT/BAE report-back for cue status, active variables, skipped bindings, capability misses, last_frame_at, and errors.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "register_webhook" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Register an HTTPS endpoint to receive signed push notifications for compel lifecycle events (compel.ready when renderable, compel.completed, compel.failed). Ret…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "register_webhook" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Register an HTTPS endpoint to receive signed push notifications for compel lifecycle events (compel.ready when renderable, compel.completed, compel.failed). Ret…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: upload_media, register_webhook, test_webhook_delivery · Private data: upload_media, search_music, create_compel_from_music, search_media, register_webhook, list_webhooks, update_webhook, rotate_webhook_secret, ack_lighting_cue · Public sink: upload_media, create_compel_from_music, register_webhook, list_webhooks, delete_webhook, test_webhook_delivery, update_webhook, rotate_webhook_secret · Destructive: delete_webhook.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: upload_media, register_webhook, test_webhook_delivery · Private data: upload_media, search_music, create_compel_from_music, search_media, register_webhook, list_webhooks, update_webhook, rotate_webhook_secret, ack_lighting_cue · Public sink: upload_media, create_compel_from_music, register_webhook, list_webhooks, delete_webhook, test_webhook_delivery, update_webhook, rotate_webhook_secret · Destructive: delete_webhook

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
